```python
# Assertion snippet for Bell-state pattern verification
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
assert total_counts == shots, f"Total counts {total_counts} must equal shots {shots}"
# Only '00' and '11' should appear
valid_outcomes = {'00', '11'}
observed_outcomes = set(counts.keys())
assert observed_outcomes.issubset(valid_outcomes), f"Invalid outcomes observed: {observed_outcomes - valid_outcomes}"
# Approximately balanced
if '00' in counts and '11' in counts:
    ratio = abs(counts['00'] - counts['11']) / shots
    threshold = 0.1  # 10% tolerance
    assert ratio <= threshold, f"Imbalance too large: {ratio:.3f} > {threshold}"
else:
    # If one outcome is missing, ensure the other has all counts (within tolerance)
    assert len(observed_outcomes) == 1, f"Expected one outcome when missing, got {len(observed_outcomes)}"
    single_outcome = next(iter(observed_outcomes))
    assert single_outcome in valid_outcomes, f"Single outcome {single_outcome} not valid"
    assert abs(counts[single_outcome] - shots) <= shots * 0.1, f"Single outcome counts {counts[single_outcome]} not close to shots {shots}"
```
